message:
fix(channel): integer-precision drift calculation and exhaustive waker cleanup on terminal paths

clock_skew: Replace floating-point drift calculation with i128 integer
arithmetic in a new compute_drift_ns() helper. The previous code
computed (drift_rate as f64 * base_nanos as f64 / 1e9) as i64, which
loses precision for base timestamps beyond 2^53 nanoseconds (~104 days)
because f64 cannot represent all integers above that threshold. The new
path computes i128(drift_rate) * i128(base_nanos) / 1_000_000_000 with
saturating multiply and clamped conversion back to i64. Adds
drift_precision_is_stable_at_large_timestamps test that uses the exact
value 9_007_199_254_740_993 (2^53+1) — not representable in f64 — and
verifies the skewed result matches the integer-exact expected value.

oneshot: Clear stale waker from inner state on every terminal RecvFuture
path, not just cancellation. Previously, successful recv (value ready)
and closed-channel recv left the registered waker alive in the channel
inner until the last Arc reference dropped, unnecessarily pinning
executor-internal memory. Now waker is set to None on value-ready and
on closed paths. Also clears waker and drops the lock early in
SendPermit::send when the receiver has already been dropped, mirroring
the success path's early-release pattern. Adds three tests:
recv_value_ready_clears_stale_waker (poll Pending, send, poll Ready,
verify waker is None), recv_closed_clears_stale_waker (poll Pending,
drop sender, poll Closed, verify waker is None), and
permit_send_receiver_dropped_clears_waker (register waker, drop
receiver, send via permit, verify no panic/deadlock).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
